title: Trump Calls Xi a ‘Friend.’ But He Left China Without Any Breakthroughs.
summary: The lack of concrete agreements with Beijing shows the risks of President Trump’s personality-driven foreign policy, which rests on the belief that he can defend U.S. interests through charm and force of will.

title: China Indicates Tariffs Were Discussed at Trump Summit
summary: China’s Ministry of Commerce said Saturday that the countries had struck a preliminary agreement to reduce some tariffs, seemingly contradicting statements by President Trump

title: China Will Host Putin, Days After Trump’s Visit
summary: The Russian leader will go to Beijing on Tuesday for talks with President Xi Jinping. The Kremlin said he had watched President Trump’s visit closely.

title: How a Nature Cruise Turned Into a Nightmare
summary: The hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius set off alarms for a world still traumatized by Covid. For those on board, the danger was much closer.

title: Voters in Louisiana Head to the Polls, Uncertain but Determined
summary: Votes cast for the House candidates on Saturday’s primary ballot won’t count after state officials moved the election to November to provide time to redraw congressional maps.

title: What to Watch in Saturday’s Republican Senate Primary in Louisiana
summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who has drawn President Trump’s ire, is fighting for political survival.

title: Bulgaria Wins Eurovision Song Contest as Israel Comes Second
summary: Five countries boycotted this year’s contest over Israel’s involvement, but politics took a back seat once the show in Vienna got underway.

title: Fears Grow That Iran May Be Using Proxy Groups Beyond Mideast
summary: The charges against Mohammad al-Saadi in the United States have raised concerns that Iran could be working with its proxies to stage attacks outside the region.

title: Hamas’s Top Leader in Gaza Is Killed in Israeli Strike
summary: Izz al-Din al-Haddad took over the group’s military wing in Gaza last year. Hamas officials confirmed Mr. al-Haddad’s death in an Israeli attack.

title: Navy Aircraft Carrier Completes Longest Deployment Since Vietnam
summary: After being diverted for combat with Venezuela and Iran, the U.S.S. Gerald R. Ford returned to Norfolk, Va., on Saturday after nearly a year at sea.

title: Stephen Colbert’s Late Show Cancellation and the Fall of CBS
summary: In shutting down a 33-year late-night franchise, CBS is assenting to its own diminishment.

title: How the Jerks I Grew Up With in Long Island Took Over the Country
summary: I noticed that a lot of TV villains come from the town I grew up in. I wasn’t surprised.

title: Car Plows Into Pedestrians in Northern Italian Town, Injuring at Least 8
summary: Four of the injured were in serious condition, the mayor of Modena said, praising residents who rushed to stop the car’s driver from escaping on foot.

title: Severe Storms to Bring Strong Winds and Hail to Central United States
summary: A series of storms, including supercells, threatens to dump rain, hail and create a tornado risk for a vast section of the country. The most severe weather is expected on Monday.

title: Mamdani’s Nakba Day Social Media Post Marks Shift for N.Y.C. Mayors
summary: Pro-Palestinian New Yorkers celebrated Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s decision to post about the Palestinians’ day of remembrance, but the move was criticized by some Jewish leaders.

title: Woman Gives Birth in Brooklyn Courtroom
summary: A woman who was nine months pregnant was in court on low-level charges, raising questions about why she was discharged from a hospital before the hearing.
